Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

commit.message:
fix: improve error semantics when payment gateway is unavailable

Distinguish between two distinct failure modes when the payment gateway
call fails after Polly retries are exhausted:

- Gateway unreachable (HttpRequestException/SocketException): payment
  transitions to Failed, orchestrator notified with status="failed" and
  reason="gateway_unavailable". Previously this was incorrectly reported
  as status="expired" which means the gateway was reached but timed out.

- Gateway timeout (TaskCanceledException): payment transitions to
  Expired, orchestrator notified with status="expired" and
  reason="gateway_timeout".

Changes:
- Add PaymentStatus.Failed enum value for connectivity failures
- Add Payment.Fail() domain method (counterpart to Expire())
- Update InitiatePaymentCommandHandler.ChargeGatewayAsync to inspect
  the inner exception type and apply the correct status/reason
- Add GatewayUnavailable and GatewayTimeout log methods to PaymentLogMessages
- Add PaymentFailedWithReason log method to OrchestratorLogMessages
- Update ProcessPaymentProcessedCommandHandler to log reason when present
- Update PaymentProcessedNotification XML docs to list all valid values
- Update ARCHITECTURE.md S2 failure description and contract section
- Add 2 unit tests covering each failure case

fix: textarea not accepting input in chat view

textarea.Focus() is a pointer-receiver method in bubbles/v2 that sets
focus=true in-place. InputModel.Init() called it on a value-receiver
copy, so the focus state was discarded — textarea.Update() then dropped
all keyboard input at its !m.focus guard.

Fix: call ta.Focus() in the NewInput() constructor where the state is
preserved. Also disable the viewport's default single-letter keybindings
(f, b, u, d, j, k, h, l, space) that would otherwise compete with the
textarea for character input.

fix: Dockerfile.ansible multi-stage build + update docs with correct RELAY_TOKEN variable

## Summary
- Updated Dockerfile.ansible to multi-stage build (GO builder → Python final image)
- Includes relay-inventory binary in container (/usr/local/bin/relay-inventory)
- Updated docker-compose.ansible.yml to reference external network (ansible_server_default)

## Changes
1. Dockerfile.ansible:
   - Stage 1: Build relay-inventory binary from GO sources
   - Stage 2: Python 3.11 + ansible-core + copy relay-inventory binary
   - Binaries: connection plugin (Python), relay-inventory (GO)

2. docker-compose.ansible.yml:
   - Added networks: ansible_server_default (external, for relay-api connectivity)
   - Updated env vars: RELAY_TOKEN, RELAY_ONLY_CONNECTED, RELAY_INSECURE_TLS
   - Removed RELAY_ADMIN_TOKEN (incorrect for inventory)

3. DEPLOYMENT/ANSIBLE_DEPLOYMENT.md:
   - Environment variables table: RELAY_TOKEN (correct), added RELAY_ONLY_CONNECTED/INSECURE_TLS
   - Usage examples updated to match

## Testing
- relay-inventory --list: ✅ Works with RELAY_TOKEN
- ansible-inventory -i /usr/local/bin/relay-inventory: ✅ Parses correctly
- 3 agents visible (qualif-host-01/02/03) with status + last_seen

## Result
- Container relay-ansible deployed on qualif with relay-inventory binary
- Fully functional inventory for Ansible playbooks
- Documentation matches actual implementation

* Add retry logic, rate limiting, per-user queue limits, and circuit breaker

- Queue retries: all queues now retry 3 times with backoff before failing
- Worker concurrency: increased defaults (TEXT=10, IMAGE=5, VIDEO=3, AUDIO=5)
- Retry-aware errors: only refund credits and notify user on final attempt,
  re-throw on earlier attempts so Bull can retry
- Per-user job limits: max 5 concurrent jobs per user per category, prevents
  single-user queue flooding
- Express rate limiting: global 100 req/min per IP, API routes 30 req/min
  per user identity (health/webhook endpoints excluded)
- Circuit breaker: providers auto-skipped for 60s after 5 consecutive
  failures, prevents wasting time on down providers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: smart polling timeout - keep waiting while task is generating

Instead of a hard 10-minute cutoff, use two-tier timeout:
- Soft timeout (10 min): give up if task is still in waiting/queuing
- Hard max (14 min): keep polling as long as task is in 'generating'
  state (actively being processed on KieAI servers)
- 14 min leaves 1 min buffer before Bull queue's 15 min job timeout

This prevents premature timeouts for complex video prompts while
still failing fast for truly stuck tasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent CDN caching of API responses causing stale settings

BunnyCDN was caching GET /api/webapp/* responses for 30 days because
neither Express nor the HTTP origin nginx blocks set Cache-Control
headers. This caused settings to appear reset on every webapp reopen
while saves appeared to work (PUTs are not cacheable).

- Add Cache-Control: no-store middleware in Express for all API routes
- Add Cache-Control: no-store to HTTP origin nginx server blocks
  (port 80, used by CDN for origin pulls)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add CDN cache-busting to frontend API requests

Append _t=timestamp to GET requests so BunnyCDN treats each
request as a unique cache key, ensuring fresh data from origin.
This works immediately without needing a CDN cache purge.

  sequences (e.g. follow-up email 7 days after onboarding)
- estado ('activa'/'inactiva'): current status for conditional automation paths

Fields tipo and ritmos remain unchanged. Sync only triggers on CREATE to
avoid re-firing the welcome automation on every edit.
